markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 137 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- Sought after by many unscrupulous bards, this instrument is surprisingly light and easy to carry, but also empowered with a number of spells carefully selected to help with fooling others or making a hasty retreat. While playing the mandolin, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. **Activate** envision; **Effect** You change the instrument’s color and shape to one you prefer, and you can turn it into a different handheld string instrument that takes two hands to play. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Trickster's Mandolin **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 137 **Price** 90 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639)
  • **1st** [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [_item facade_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579), [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740)
Trickster's Mandolin (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 137 **Price** 460 gp ---
  • **2nd** i[_llusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [_mirror image _](/Spells.aspx?ID=197)
  • **3rd** _illusory disguise_, [_phantom prison_](/Spells.aspx?ID=969), [_sculpt sound_](/Spells.aspx?ID=714)
Trickster's Mandolin (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 137 **Price** 1,900 gp --- The item bonuses are +2.
  • **4th** [_confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), _invisibility_, [_veil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568)
  • **5th** [_hallucination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1551), [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), _veil_
name: Trickster's Mandolin (Greater)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Wand of Choking Mist](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4806)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 138

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This blackened wood wand has a smoldering tip, emitting a slight trail of steam.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_chromatic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=881) of the indicated level. Additionally, the target can use the Chromatic Armor Burst action.

**Chromatic Armor** Burst <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)); **Requirements** You’re affected by _chromatic armor_ created by the _wand of chromatic burst_; **Effect** Choose one color of the _chromatic armor_ the wand created for you. The spell ends and light of that color flashes brightly in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Creatures in the area take damage of the type associated with the color you chose, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against your spell DC; the amount of damage depends on the wand’s type. This action has the trait corresponding to the damage type you chose.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_obscuring mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=210) or [_solid fog_](/Spells.aspx?ID=290).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Wand of Choking Mist (2nd-Level Spell)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 138

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The spell is [_obscuring mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=210).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Wand of Choking Mist (4th-Level Spell)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 138

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The spell is [_solid fog_](/Spells.aspx?ID=290).
name: Wand of Choking Mist

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Contagious Frailty](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4809)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 139

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Cracks and healed fractures spiderweb the shaft of this bone wand, worsening each time the wand is used. The bone’s worn epiphysis forms the wand’s pommel, and black leather wraps around the handle.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_ray of enfeeblement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=244). After you cast the spell, if the target is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), it releases a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) that doesn't include itself. Each creature in that area must attempt a Fortitude save as if struck by the _ray of enfeeblement_ but gets an outcome one degree of success better than it rolled.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_ray of enfeeblement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=244).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Contagious Frailty

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Crushing Leaps](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4810)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 139

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This supple, light wooden wand drifts to the ground like a feather or leaf when dropped, landing unharmed. A thin coil of metal wraps around the wand’s handle.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_jump_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1580), but can jump up to 60 feet. When you land you shatter the ground, making each creature in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of its next turn. In addition, the space you land in and all squares in the emanation become [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for 1 minute.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of 1st-level [_jump_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1580).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Crushing Leaps

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Dazzling Rays](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4811)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 139

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Solidified radiance comprises this slender, featureless wand. It sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet. After you Activate the wand, the light fades, so it only sheds dim light in a 20-foot radius. The wand returns to its original brightness each dawn.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast searing light of the indicated level, dazzling your target with the beam’s intensity. A creature that takes damage from the spell is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for a number of rounds equal to the spell level. On a critical success on the attack roll, the target is also blinded for as long as it’s dazzled from the spell. However, it can attempt a Fortitude saving throw against your spell DC at the end of each of its turns, ending the blinded condition on a success (but remaining dazzled).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_searing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=269) of the appropriate level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Dazzling Rays](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4811)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 139

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Solidified radiance comprises this slender, featureless wand. It sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet. After you Activate the wand, the light fades, so it only sheds dim light in a 20-foot radius. The wand returns to its original brightness each dawn.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast searing light of the indicated level, dazzling your target with the beam’s intensity. A creature that takes damage from the spell is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for a number of rounds equal to the spell level. On a critical success on the attack roll, the target is also blinded for as long as it’s dazzled from the spell. However, it can attempt a Fortitude saving throw against your spell DC at the end of each of its turns, ending the blinded condition on a success (but remaining dazzled).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_searing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=269) of the appropriate level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Dazzling Rays](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4811)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 139

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Solidified radiance comprises this slender, featureless wand. It sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet. After you Activate the wand, the light fades, so it only sheds dim light in a 20-foot radius. The wand returns to its original brightness each dawn.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast searing light of the indicated level, dazzling your target with the beam’s intensity. A creature that takes damage from the spell is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for a number of rounds equal to the spell level. On a critical success on the attack roll, the target is also blinded for as long as it’s dazzled from the spell. However, it can attempt a Fortitude saving throw against your spell DC at the end of each of its turns, ending the blinded condition on a success (but remaining dazzled).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_searing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=269) of the appropriate level.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Dumbfounding Doom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4812)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Incapacitation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=93" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Prediction" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=132" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 139

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carvings of skulls, monsters, and all manner of violence decorate this wand of blackened bone, but it makes absurd sounds when Activated, such as a honking horn, a manic giggle, or a daydreamy sigh.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_impending doom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=929) of the indicated level, showing the target a potential death that’s gruesome and absurd. If the target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) by the spell, it also becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) with a value 1 higher than the frightened value. This lasts for the duration of the spell.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_impending doom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=929) of the appropriate level.

</column>

**Bulk** L
</row>

---

Carvings of skulls, monsters, and all manner of violence decorate this wand of blackened bone, but it makes absurd sounds when Activated, such as a honking horn, a manic giggle, or a daydreamy sigh.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_impending doom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=929) of the indicated level, showing the target a potential death that’s gruesome and absurd. If the target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) by the spell, it also becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) with a value 1 higher than the frightened value. This lasts for the duration of the spell.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_impending doom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=929) of the appropriate level.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Dumbfounding Doom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4812)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Incapacitation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=93" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Prediction" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=132" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 139

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carvings of skulls, monsters, and all manner of violence decorate this wand of blackened bone, but it makes absurd sounds when Activated, such as a honking horn, a manic giggle, or a daydreamy sigh.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_impending doom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=929) of the indicated level, showing the target a potential death that’s gruesome and absurd. If the target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) by the spell, it also becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) with a value 1 higher than the frightened value. This lasts for the duration of the spell.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_impending doom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=929) of the appropriate level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Hawthorn](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4813)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved from a hawthorn branch, this wand has a smooth handle, but the shaft remains covered in bark and long thorns. Polished red stones, arranged like a cluster of berries, decorate the pommel.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_barkskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=20) of the indicated level, and the target sprouts long thorns like those of a hawthorn tree. While barkskin lasts, any creature that hits the target with an unarmed Strike or otherwise touches it takes piercing damage from the thorns, with the amount determined by the wand's type. A creature that has [engulfed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=61) or [swallowed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) the target takes this damage as well at the start of each of the target's turns.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_barkskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=20) of the appropriate level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

<row gap="medium">

The thorns deal 1d4 piercing damage.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The grain of this simple wooden wand forms shifting images of sharp claws, snapping jaws, and countless creatures.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; This activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, selecting two forms from among those you can normally choose. You gain the benefits of both forms. For example, if one form can breathe air and the other can breathe underwater, you can breathe in both situations. If there’s overlap in abilities, you gain the better one. For instance, if both have a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350), you get the higher one, and if both forms have claws, you gain only the claw Strike you prefer. The GM determines which abilities overlap and which are cumulative.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate level. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, must have the [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) trait, and must allow more than one choice of battle form.

You gain the benefits of both forms. For example, if one form can breathe air and the other can breathe underwater, you can breathe in both situations. If there’s overlap in abilities, you gain the better one. For instance, if both have a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350), you get the higher one, and if both forms have claws, you gain only the claw Strike you prefer. The GM determines which abilities overlap and which are cumulative.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate level. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, must have the [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) trait, and must allow more than one choice of battle form.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Legerdemain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4815)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand of lacquered black wood has a handle wrapped in interwoven colorful ribbons. A silver bell caps the wand’s tasseled pommel.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Interact ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727))   **Requirements** The last action you took this turn was to Cast a Spell from the wand; **Effect** You make yourself the center of attention. An illusory spotlight shines [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) upon your space as you pull inane objects from the wand's tip, such as confetti, silk flowers, streamers, or a long string of colorful kerchiefs knotted end to end. Each enemy within 30 feet must attempt a Will save against your spell DC, receiving a +4 circumstance bonus to the save if you or any of your allies recently threatened it or used hostile actions against it. On a failure, the creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with you until the end of your next turn. The fascination ends if the target is subject to a [hostile act](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251), or if another creature succeeds at a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check against it.

The spotlight follows you wherever you move. You can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) while in the spotlight. The effect ends if you become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) check, or [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the activation.

You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) this Activation for up to 1 minute. Since you need to keep performing tricks, Sustain an Activation has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. Sustaining extends the spotlight, and keeps fascinated creatures fascinated, but doesn't cause creatures not already fascinated to become fascinated.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate level.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Legerdemain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4815)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand of lacquered black wood has a handle wrapped in interwoven colorful ribbons. A silver bell caps the wand’s tasseled pommel.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Interact ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727))   **Requirements** The last action you took this turn was to Cast a Spell from the wand; **Effect** You make yourself the center of attention. An illusory spotlight shines [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) upon your space as you pull inane objects from the wand's tip, such as confetti, silk flowers, streamers, or a long string of colorful kerchiefs knotted end to end. Each enemy within 30 feet must attempt a Will save against your spell DC, receiving a +4 circumstance bonus to the save if you or any of your allies recently threatened it or used hostile actions against it. On a failure, the creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with you until the end of your next turn. The fascination ends if the target is subject to a [hostile act](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251), or if another creature succeeds at a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check against it.

The spotlight follows you wherever you move. You can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) while in the spotlight. The effect ends if you become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) check, or [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the activation.

You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) this Activation for up to 1 minute. Since you need to keep performing tricks, Sustain an Activation has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. Sustaining extends the spotlight, and keeps fascinated creatures fascinated, but doesn't cause creatures not already fascinated to become fascinated.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate level.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Legerdemain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4815)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand of lacquered black wood has a handle wrapped in interwoven colorful ribbons. A silver bell caps the wand’s tasseled pommel.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Interact ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727))   **Requirements** The last action you took this turn was to Cast a Spell from the wand; **Effect** You make yourself the center of attention. An illusory spotlight shines [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) upon your space as you pull inane objects from the wand's tip, such as confetti, silk flowers, streamers, or a long string of colorful kerchiefs knotted end to end. Each enemy within 30 feet must attempt a Will save against your spell DC, receiving a +4 circumstance bonus to the save if you or any of your allies recently threatened it or used hostile actions against it. On a failure, the creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with you until the end of your next turn. The fascination ends if the target is subject to a [hostile act](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251), or if another creature succeeds at a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check against it.

The spotlight follows you wherever you move. You can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) while in the spotlight. The effect ends if you become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) check, or [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the activation.

You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) this Activation for up to 1 minute. Since you need to keep performing tricks, Sustain an Activation has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. Sustaining extends the spotlight, and keeps fascinated creatures fascinated, but doesn't cause creatures not already fascinated to become fascinated.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate level.

</column>

Red feathers hang from the handle of this ivory wand. Holding it brings a sense of gentle calm.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_soothe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1678) of the indicated level, and can attempt to counteract one [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect on the same target. Treat the soothe spell’s level as 1 higher for this counteract check.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_soothe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1678) of the appropriate level.

<title

>
[Wand of Mercy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4817)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The pommel of this rose quartz wand resembles the stylized wings of an [angel](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=359). When you cast its spell and choose not to make it [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), the crystal deepens to blood red. The color reverts to rose when you cast the spell from the wand nonlethally.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; the activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, and can choose to give it the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate level. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, it must deal damage, and it can’t have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), negative, or [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) traits.

[Wand of Mercy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4817)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 141

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The pommel of this rose quartz wand resembles the stylized wings of an [angel](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=359). When you cast its spell and choose not to make it [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), the crystal deepens to blood red. The color reverts to rose when you cast the spell from the wand nonlethally.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; the activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, and can choose to give it the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate level. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, it must deal damage, and it can’t have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), negative, or [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) traits.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell; the activation takes <actions string="Two Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Single Action" /> to cast, or <actions string="Three Actions" /> if the spell normally takes <actions string="Two Actions" />;;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell, and can choose to give it the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of a spell of the appropriate level. The spell must have a casting time of <actions string="Single Action" /> or <actions string="Two Actions" />, it must deal damage, and it can’t have the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), negative, or [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) traits.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Paralytic Shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4819)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 141

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A two-pronged metal rod, this wand has a handle coated in thick rubber. Each prong ends in a copper coil. When Activated, the wand produces a loud zapping sound.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), electrocuting the target into immobility. Each target takes electricity damage at the start of its turns while it remains [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) or [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) due to the spell. The amount of damage depends on the wand’s type.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) of the appropriate level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wand of Paralytic Shock (3rd-Level Spell)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 141

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The electrocution deals 1d12 electricity damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wand of Paralytic Shock (7th-Level Spell)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 141

**Price** 10,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The electrocution deals 2d12 electricity damage.
name: Wand of Paralytic Shock

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Paralytic Shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4819)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 141

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A two-pronged metal rod, this wand has a handle coated in thick rubber. Each prong ends in a copper coil. When Activated, the wand produces a loud zapping sound.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), electrocuting the target into immobility. Each target takes electricity damage at the start of its turns while it remains [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) or [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) due to the spell. The amount of damage depends on the wand’s type.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) of the appropriate level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Refracting Rays](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4822)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 142

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand is short and wide with a hexagonal, crystal shaft and a leather-wrapped handle. The wand refracts direct bright light into a rainbow.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_chromatic ray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=883) of the indicated level. After you cast the spell, if you hit the target, the light refracts to another creature within 30 feet of the first target. Roll your spell attack roll and to determine the ray's color separately for each target. The ray continues to refract each time it hits. The refraction ceases if you miss any target, and you can end the refraction at any point. You can't target the same creature more than once, and you must have line of effect to all targets.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_chromatic ray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=883) of the appropriate level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 1,400 gp

</row>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Rolling Flames](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4823)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 142

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The luminous design of red-orange cracks on this black obsidian wand suggests cooling lava.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_flaming sphere_](/Spells.aspx?ID=121) of the indicated level. The ground in the sphere's square and all adjacent squares are coated in rolling flames until the start of your next turn. These are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368). A creature that moves on the ground takes fire damage for every square of rolling flames it moves into, with the amount determined by the wand's type. If a creature in the flames doesn't move on its turn, it takes the damage for each of the squares it's in at the end of its turn. The first time you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the Spell each round, the sphere creates rolling flames again in its new location (or the same location if you chose not to move it).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_flaming sphere_](/Spells.aspx?ID=121) of the appropriate level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wand of Rolling Flames (2nd-level spell)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 142

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The rolling flames deal 1 fire damage.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"

[Wand of Rolling Flames](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4823)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 142

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The luminous design of red-orange cracks on this black obsidian wand suggests cooling lava.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_flaming sphere_](/Spells.aspx?ID=121) of the indicated level. The ground in the sphere's square and all adjacent squares are coated in rolling flames until the start of your next turn. These are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368). A creature that moves on the ground takes fire damage for every square of rolling flames it moves into, with the amount determined by the wand's type. If a creature in the flames doesn't move on its turn, it takes the damage for each of the squares it's in at the end of its turn. The first time you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the Spell each round, the sphere creates rolling flames again in its new location (or the same location if you chose not to move it).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_flaming sphere_](/Spells.aspx?ID=121) of the appropriate level.

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 142

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The luminous design of red-orange cracks on this black obsidian wand suggests cooling lava.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_flaming sphere_](/Spells.aspx?ID=121) of the indicated level. The ground in the sphere's square and all adjacent squares are coated in rolling flames until the start of your next turn. These are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2368). A creature that moves on the ground takes fire damage for every square of rolling flames it moves into, with the amount determined by the wand's type. If a creature in the flames doesn't move on its turn, it takes the damage for each of the squares it's in at the end of its turn. The first time you [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the Spell each round, the sphere creates rolling flames again in its new location (or the same location if you chose not to move it).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_flaming sphere_](/Spells.aspx?ID=121) of the appropriate level.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Shattering Images](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4824)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 142

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Shattered pieces of a glass mirror, formed into a reflective mosaic, comprise the irregular shaft of this wand. The handle is black and polished smooth, the pommel capped with a tiny reflective orb.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197). Each time an image is destroyed, the image shatters into sharp fragments of glass. If the creature that destroyed the image is adjacent to you, it takes 5 slashing damage.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Shattering Images

<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 142

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you move this delicately carved poplar wand, it looks indistinct, leaving a trail of afterimages in its wake.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_fleet step_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531). For the duration of the spell, you’re also [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) while you [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_fleet step_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Shrouded Step

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Teeming Ghosts](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4826)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 142

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pale wooden wand is carved to resemble a thigh bone with metal caps at each end. Ghostly tendrils seem to swirl around it every so often.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_false life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=108) of the indicated level. During the duration of the spell, wisps that resemble spirits flit around you as long as you have any [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) from _false life_, and you can use the following action.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;   **Trigger** You successfully impart the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 condition on a creature; **Requirements** You have at least 1 [temporary Hit Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) from _false life_; **Effect** You end _false life_ and increase the creature’s frightened condition value to 2.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_false life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=108) of the appropriate level.

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pale wooden wand is carved to resemble a thigh bone with metal caps at each end. Ghostly tendrils seem to swirl around it every so often.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_false life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=108) of the indicated level. During the duration of the spell, wisps that resemble spirits flit around you as long as you have any [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) from _false life_, and you can use the following action.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;   **Trigger** You successfully impart the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 condition on a creature; **Requirements** You have at least 1 [temporary Hit Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) from _false life_; **Effect** You end _false life_ and increase the creature’s frightened condition value to 2.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_false life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=108) of the appropriate level.

>
[Wand of the Ash Puppet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4827)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 143

**Price** 4,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand is composed of ash that has been compressed, shaped, and sealed with a clear lacquer. When you trace the wand’s tip along a solid surface, it leaves a black trail of charcoal. Writing with the wand in this way never damages or wears the wand down.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492). If the spell reduces a living creature to fine powder, you animate that creature’s ashes into a [sulfur zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1377) with the same general appearance as the disintegrated creature. You control this sulfur zombie, which gains the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) and [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=706) traits. You can issue a verbal command to the sulfur zombie as a single action with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) and [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) traits. The sulfur zombie crumbles into inanimate ash when reduced to 0 Hit Points or after 1 minute, whichever comes first.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of the Ash Puppet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of the Pampered Pet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4828)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 143

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This extravagant wand is made of gold and capped with a large, sparkling gemstone. Its handle is wrapped in plush, padded fabric.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_pet cache_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1627) but the accommodations inside the extradimensional space are luxurious and spacious. The food is delicious gourmet cuisine tailored to the pet’s palate, the habitat is the perfect temperature and environment for the pet, complete with comfortable bed or lounging area. A pair of phantom hands pamper the pet, patting, grooming, or playing with it at the creature’s whim.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_pet cache_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1627).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of the Pampered Pet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Tormented Slumber](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4829)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Sleep" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=145" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 143

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The carved talon of an unidentifiable beast comprises this wand. Blood-stained cloth wraps the thicker part of the talon, which acts as a handle.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast 4th-level [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675), but the slumbering creatures have terrifying nightmares. A creature knocked unconscious by this spell takes 1d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). This damage wakes the creature from [unconsciousness](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) only if it deals 4 or more damage on a single roll. If the creature awakens from its unconsciousness due to damage (whether it was the persistent mental damage or not), it’s [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1. If it awakens from damage on its own turn, the creature doesn’t reduce its frightened condition automatically on that turn.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of 4th-level [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Tormented Slumber

[Wand of Traitorous Thoughts](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4831)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you hold this sleek, shiny gray wand, you hear a faint chorus of overlapping whispers.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_mind probe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601). The first time the target rolls a success at a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check to mislead your probe, it gets a result one step worse than it rolled. This means you learn the answer if the target’s Deception check would have succeeded, and you learn nothing rather than believing a falsehood if the check would have been a critical success.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_mind probe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Menacing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4833)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied any visible article of clothing

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Common among brutes who use the magic to scare others into compliance, menacing runes lend you a formidable appearance, granting you a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) others.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Menacing
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Menacing (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This rune grants a +2 item bonus and has an activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The rune casts a 3rd-level [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) spell (DC 25).
name: Menacing

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pontoon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4834)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to footwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Footwear with pontoon runes allows you to traverse waterways with ease. While wearing footwear with the rune, you can walk on the surface of water and other liquids without falling through. This offers no protection against damage the liquid deals. You can go under the liquid’s surface, but you must [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) if you do.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pontoon

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Preserving](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4835)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to a basket, bag, or other container

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Preserving_ runes are common among merchants and other travelers who are on the road for weeks or months at a time. Any non-magical food and drink inside a container with a _preserving_ rune remains fresher for longer, taking ten times as long to spoil. This feature doesn’t prolong the duration of alchemical items.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Preserving
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

**Price** 45 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Preserving (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 144

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Non-magical food and drink inside the container never spoil, and the rune has an activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The container casts [_purify food and drink_](/Spells.aspx?ID=241) on all the food and drink within.
name: Preserving

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Trackless](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4836)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 145

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to footwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Trackless_ runes are common among hunters and trappers, as well as thieves and anyone fleeing pursuit. While wearing trackless footwear, you have a +4 item bonus to the DC to [track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) you. However, this bonus doesn't stack with the status bonus from [_pass without trace_](/Spells.aspx?ID=215).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Trackless
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 145

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Trackless (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 145

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The rune has an activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You extend the effect of your rune out to a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). The emanation remains for 8 hours, affecting up to 10 creatures of your choice within the area. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this effect.
name: Trackless

[Alchemist's Haversack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4837)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 145

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn backpack

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _alchemist’s haversack_ is a sturdy leather backpack with two compartments. The main section contains an extradimensional space equivalent to a bag of holding type II, perfect for carrying bulkier alchemist equipment. A secondary partition can hold 2 Bulk of items, 1 of which doesn’t count against your Bulk limit. This second compartment can also be activated (see below). 

Two shoulder straps and a thick belt for the waist hold the haversack on the wearer securely. These straps have pouches and loops for keeping alchemical items handy. They also contain magically expanded alchemist’s tools that grant you a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks to create alchemical items. 

Water and undesirable material can’t seep into the haversack, which cleans itself once per hour. When you reach in any part of the haversack, the item you sought is the first one you find. Also, the haversack preserves mundane ingredients, food, and drink inside, so they stay fresh indefinitely. This feature doesn’t prolong the duration of magic or alchemical items.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You gain batches of infused reagents during your daily preparations; **Effect** You pull one additional batch of infused reagents from the satchel’s secondary compartment. If you fail to use these reagents by the end of your next turn, they’re lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are an alchemist.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Alchemist's Haversack

[Everyneed Pack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4838)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 145

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn backpack

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Constructed of green material and decorated with a white Glyph of the Open Road, an everyneed pack has a dozen or more small pockets lining the inside. The pack is enchanted so that each pocket contains common, mundane gear, each item worth no more than 1 gp, such as chalk, flint and steel, and string. It doesn’t contain any armor, shields, weapons, or items made of precious material. Keep track of the exact value of the gear you retrieve from the pack. The pack becomes a mundane backpack after items of your choice with a combined value of 8 gp have been removed from it.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (Interact);  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You draw any number of pieces of mundane gear from the pack with a combined value of 1 gp or less.

</column>

[Vaultbreaker's Harness](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4839)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 145

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn backpack

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _vaultbreaker’s harness_ has four pockets across the chest. The pockets contain a set of [infiltrator’s thieves’ tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758), infiltrator picks, a levered crowbar, and a glass cutter. These items are magically bound to the harness; if they are more than 1 foot away from you, they disappear, then reappear in the harness at the next sunset. Broken or destroyed items similarly reappear, restored, in their proper pockets at sunset.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You cinch the harness to prepare for mischief. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks and a +10-foot item bonus to your Speed for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vaultbreaker's Harness

    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bellflower Toolbelt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4840)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 145

**Price** 220 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Different versions of the _bellflower toolbelt_ are customized to appear to suit specific trades, so a belt used for carpentry would look different from a belt for baking.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;     **Effect** You place an object of up to 1 Bulk into the belt, transforming that object into a tool befitting the trade for which the belt was created. Each object remains transformed until it has been removed from the belt for 24 hours or someone uses a single [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to return it to its normal form. If enough transformed items are in it, the belt can be used as [artisan’s tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703) for that trade.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bellflower Toolbelt

<title

    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"

[Bravery Baldric](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4841)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 155

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _bravery baldric_ is a belt that wraps around the shoulder and draws on your well of courage. When you critically succeed on a save against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effect or reduce your [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition to 0, the baldric gains 1 charge, which slightly alters the color. A _bravery baldric_ can hold up to 2 charges, and its charges reset to 0 when you invest it. You can have only one _bravery baldric_ invested at a time.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;    **Requirements** The baldric has a charge; **Effect** One charge in the baldric expires, and you gain its benefit, according to its type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bravery Baldric (Fleet)

</traits>

</column>

---

The baldric is grass green when charged, and it grants you the effects of [_fleet step_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531). This baldric has the [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) trait.

---

The baldric is light blue when charged, and it grants you the effects of [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534). This baldric has the [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) trait.

level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bravery Baldric (Haste)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 146

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The baldric is silver when charged, and it grants you the effects of [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553). This baldric has the [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) trait.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"

>
Bravery Baldric (Restoration)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

The baldric is golden when charged, and it grants you the effects of a 2nd-level [_restoration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=258). This baldric has the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) and [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) traits.

The baldric is dark gray when charged, and it grants you the effects of [_stoneskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=312). This baldric has the [abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2) trait.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bravery Baldric](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4841)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 155

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _bravery baldric_ is a belt that wraps around the shoulder and draws on your well of courage. When you critically succeed on a save against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effect or reduce your [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition to 0, the baldric gains 1 charge, which slightly alters the color. A _bravery baldric_ can hold up to 2 charges, and its charges reset to 0 when you invest it. You can have only one _bravery baldric_ invested at a time.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;    **Requirements** The baldric has a charge; **Effect** One charge in the baldric expires, and you gain its benefit, according to its type.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clockwork Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4842)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 146

**Price** 20,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Paper-thin interlocking cogs and gears make up the bronze _clockwork cloak_.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You’re struck by a melee attack with a held weapon;  **Effect** The folds of the cloak attempt to divert the attack and catch the weapon in the cloak’s gears. Make an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) the attacking creature.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You wrap the cloak around yourself and the winding gears decelerate your body, causing you to enter standby mode. While in standby mode you don’t need to eat, drink, or sleep. You remain aware of your surroundings but take a –4 penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks. You can stay in standby mode indefinitely, although your body ages normally. You can leave standby mode as a free action. If you do so to initiate combat, you gain a +2 item bonus to your initiative roll.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Clockwork Cloak

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sure-Step Crampons](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4844)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 146

**Price** 210 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Sure-step crampons_ are sturdy leather boots with warm fur lining and magically augmented steel crampons that offer the wearer better purchase on ice. They allow you to walk across ice without difficulty, [ignoring the uneven ground and difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) caused by ice, and reducing greater difficult terrain caused by ice to difficult terrain.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;    **Requirements** You’re standing on an earthen, icy, or wooden surface; **Effect** You dig the crampons into the spot where you’re standing, offering additional support until the next time you move. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude and Reflex DCs against attempts to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against spells or effects that attempt to move you or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The bonus lasts until you move from your current spot.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sure-Step Crampons

    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thrower's Bandolier](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4845)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 146

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bandolier is covered in straps and pouches capable of holding up to 2 Bulk of one-handed thrown weapons. A thrower’s bandolier has a [_+1 weapon potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) rune etched into it, and it can be etched with runes as though it were a one-handed thrown weapon. When you invest the _thrower’s bandolier_, you can attune it to all the weapons sheathed in it (this ends any previous attunements made with the bandolier). Whenever you draw a weapon from the bandolier, the bandolier’s runes are replicated onto that weapon. Any runes already on the weapon are suppressed, and any runes previously replicated to a different weapon in this way are removed, returning it to normal.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;     **Effect** All weapons attuned to the bandolier, not including any weapons you’re currently wielding, return to the bandolier.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Thrower's Bandolier

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clarity Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4846)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 146

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The counteract check from a pair of _clarity goggles_ has a counteract rank of half the item’s level rounded up._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Clarity goggles_ feature faceted lenses that filter your surroundings from several slightly different angles at once, giving you a sharper picture of them. While wearing the goggles, you gain a +1 item bonus to visual [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You focus on your environment and the creatures around you to see them as they really are. The GM rolls a secret [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check using your Perception bonus against any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) effect created by a 3rd-level or lower spell or a creature of 8th level or lower. You must be able to see the illusion, and it must be within 60 feet. If the check succeeds, you see through the illusion for 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spectacles of Inquiry](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4848)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 147

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Anything viewed through these thin spectacles looks crisp and clear, and the earpieces accentuate sounds around you. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The spectacles key in on someone to show you their social cues in perfect clarity. Choose a creature you can see. You gain a +3 item bonus on [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks you make to use [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) against that creature. This benefit lasts until you Activate the item again in this way. 

If you’re an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=59), you can use this activation as part of the action you take to [Devise a Stratagem](/Actions.aspx?ID=2813) or [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=2811), and you can do the latter even though you can’t see the creature. You must choose the same creature you chose for Devise a Stratagem or Pursue a Lead.

**Craft Requirements** You are an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=59).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectacles of Inquiry

    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spectacles of Piercing Sight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4849)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 147

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

With lenses set in a silver frame, spectacles of piercing sight grant you a +3 item bonus to [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You can see into and through solid matter for 1 minute. This vision can pierce through solid materials up to 20 feet away as if looking at something in normal light even if no illumination is available. You can see through up to 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of metal, or 3 feet of wood or dirt. A thin sheet of lead blocks this vision entirely.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectacles of Piercing Sight

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Accompaniment Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4850)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 147

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lush velvet capelet is embroidered with images of musicians playing a wide variety of instruments. The images animate when you make art, remaining embroidered, but smoothly unknitting and reknitting in time with your performance. The figures play music that perfectly accompanies your instrument, voice, or movements, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=32) [composition](/Traits.aspx?ID=559) spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You peel the musicians from the garment and fling them around you. The cloak casts a 4th-level [_phantom crowd_](/Spells.aspx?ID=968) spell (DC 28); each of the 10‑foot squares must be adjacent to you. The crowd looks like the musicians on the garment and continues to accompany your [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this effect as described in the spell.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=32).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Accompaniment Cloak
